metadata.summary: Seven frameworks for solopreneur ideas: skills, pain mining, job substitution, service, unbundling, stacking, own problems. Score candidates on 5-criterion matrix before niche-evaluation.

content.title: Idea Generation for Solopreneurs
content.summary: Seven systematic frameworks for discovering solopreneur business ideas: skills inventory, pain-point mining, job substitution, productized service, unbundling, market stacking, and own problems. Each framework produces candidate ideas; all candidates are scored on a weighted 5-criterion matrix (market size 20%, personal fit 25%, competition 15%, monetization 20%, speed-to-MVP 20%) before handoff to niche-evaluation. Waiting for inspiration produces mode-collapsed output: "AI-powered X", "task tracker for Y". The seven frameworks force different ideation angles and surface ideas grounded in actual skills and observed pains. Scoring before choosing prevents gut-feel selection that ignores market or fit signals. The two-pass diverge/converge pattern — generate 30+ candidates, then score top 5 — avoids premature convergence and fabricated scoring.

why.p: Waiting for inspiration produces mode-collapsed output: "AI-powered X", "task tracker for Y". The seven frameworks force different ideation angles and surface ideas grounded in actual skills and observed pains. Scoring before choosing prevents gut-feel selection that ignores market or fit signals.

The two-pass diverge/converge pattern — generate 30+ candidates, then score top 5 — avoids premature convergence and fabricated scoring. LLM idea generation is mode-collapsed: same prompt produces same 30 ideas across solopreneur literature. Pain-point mining via Reddit/forums requires actual scraping; without WebFetch the agent fabricates plausible complaints. No personal-fit signal exists: agent doesn't know your skill depth, only what you list. Output skews toward what's lexically common in training data (SaaS), undercounting service and info-product opportunities.

list.item: Solopreneur stuck on "what to build" with skills inventory but no candidate list.
Need 20-50 raw candidates fast across 7 frameworks for downstream niche-evaluation scoring.
Refresh of stale roadmap: feed weekly capture template into agent on cron.
Already have a validated idea with paying customers — skip to pricing-research or mvp-scoping.
Generating ideas without skills/constraints input — agents produce generic, ungrounded lists.
Scientific or research-domain novelty needed — LLMs converge on consensus; use literature-driven discovery.

section.name: seven-frameworks
section.p: Seven systematic frameworks for discovering solopreneur business ideas:

list.item: Skills Inventory: map technical, domain, and soft skills; ask "what problems can I solve with these?"
Pain Point Mining: scan Reddit top posts, app store 1-2 star reviews, Twitter rants, Quora questions for recurring complaints.
Job Substitution: list tasks done repeatedly on Upwork/Fiverr; formula: "Automate [task] for [audience]".
Productized Service: find a repeatable service; package the repeatable part as a product (consulting to course, custom dev to SaaS template).
Unbundling: break large platforms into focused tools ("what do people use Excel/Notion/Salesforce for that deserves its own tool?").
Market Stacking: cross two niches to find an underserved intersection (AI x Legal = contract analysis; Fitness x Remote = home workout for devs).
Own Problems: document daily frustrations; if you have the problem, others likely do too.

rule.statement: Require evidence URLs for pain-point mining output. Without source URLs, agent-generated complaints are fabrication from training data.
rule.rationale: LLMs produce plausible-sounding complaints without any grounding in actual user posts. Requiring URL evidence forces real source retrieval and eliminates confabulation.

example.title: Pain Point Template with Evidence
example.code: Pain Point: [What people complain about] Source: [URL to actual Reddit/forum post] Frequency: [How often mentioned in that community] Current Solutions: [What exists today] Gap: [What is missing] Idea: [Your solution]

example.title: Market Stacking Examples
example.code: AI x Legal = Contract analysis tool (LexisNexis, Ironclad) Fitness x Remote work = Home workout app for developers Crypto x Freelancing = Payment platform (Request Finance) E-commerce x Automation = Price monitoring SaaS

rule.statement: Run divergent ideation (30+ candidates across all 7 frameworks) in one context and convergence scoring in a separate context. Never let the same agent that generated ideas also score them.
rule.rationale: The generating agent rationalizes its output and will score its own ideas higher. Separation is the only reliable way to get unbiased scoring.

section.name: scoring-matrix
section.p: Rate each idea 1-5 on five weighted criteria:

list.item: Market Size (20%): 5 = billion-dollar TAM, 1 = tiny niche
Personal Fit (25%): 5 = excited to work on it daily, 1 = dreading the work
Competition (15%): 5 = blue ocean, 1 = dominated by giants
Monetization (20%): 5 = clear revenue path, 1 = unclear how to charge

rule.statement: Override the weighted total score when Personal Fit is below 3. A 4.0-fit/3-market idea ships; a 2-fit/5-market idea dies. Personal fit is the highest-weight single criterion for solopreneurs.
rule.rationale: Solopreneurs cannot afford to work on ideas they are not genuinely interested in — they lack the team structure that sustains motivation through hard periods. Fit below 3 predicts abandonment regardless of market signal.

rule.statement: Hard-cap Speed to MVP score at 3 for any idea touching auth, billing, or third-party integrations. Agents systematically over-rate this criterion, claiming everything is 2 weeks.
rule.rationale: Auth, billing, and integrations are the three most common scope underestimation sources. They reliably extend timelines by 2-4x regardless of apparent technical simplicity.

example.title: Worked Scoring Example
example.code:
Skills: Python, APIs, web scraping Source: Reddit r/startups - "monitoring competitor prices is tedious" (N=47 upvotes) Framework: Pain Mining + Market Stacking (E-commerce x Automation) Idea: Price monitoring SaaS for e-commerce stores
Scores: - Market Size: 4 (e-commerce monitoring is established category) - Personal Fit: 5 (genuinely likes building scrapers) - Competition: 3 (fragmented; no dominant player for SMBs) - Monetization: 5 (clear SaaS model, comparable tools at $49-199/mo) - Speed to MVP: 3 (scraping + billing = 3-4 weeks minimum) Weighted Total: 4.0 → Proceed to niche-evaluation

rule.statement: Run idea generation on a weekly schedule with fresh inputs (Reddit scrape, week's personal pains). Archive monthly; most useful ideas compound across months, not sessions.
rule.rationale: A single ideation session produces a biased snapshot. Weekly runs with fresh inputs surface emerging patterns and reduce anchoring to the first batch of ideas.

rule.statement: Seed with constraints, not goals: "I have 10h/week, $0 budget, Python + B2B SaaS background" beats "find me a billion-dollar idea".
rule.rationale: Constraints are idea generators, not limiters. Specific constraints surface opportunities the generic prompt never reaches.

rule.statement: Run weekly, archive monthly: ideas compound. Most useful idea at month 3 is rarely the one from week 1.
rule.rationale: Recurring ideation surfaces emerging patterns across weeks.

rule.statement: Score independently from generation: same agent that generated will rationalize bad ideas. Use second context.
rule.rationale: Agent rationalizes its own output; separation forces unbiased evaluation.

rule.statement: Personal fit > market: a 4.0-fit/3-market idea ships; a 2-fit/5-market idea dies. Override the weighted total when fit < 3.
rule.rationale: Fit determines execution; market alone does not.

rule.statement: Cap scoring at 5 candidates: scoring 30 is theatre. Filter to top-by-gut, then score rigorously.
rule.rationale: Excessive scoring reduces decision quality; focus on the most promising subset.

rule.statement: Disambiguate framework-stacking: market-stacking + unbundling generates the same idea twice. Dedupe by audience+problem, not by name.
rule.rationale: Deduplication prevents false richness in the candidate list.

section.name: ai-agent-gotchas

rule.statement: Forbid "AI", "smart", "intelligent", and "GPT" as prefixes or suffixes in the diverge prompt. Require each framework to produce at least one non-software idea.
rule.rationale: LLMs converge on these labels without explicit prohibition. Forcing non-software ideas surfaces service, content, and physical product opportunities that are often undervalued.

antipattern.description: Agent produces "AI-powered X", "smart Y for developers" across all 7 frameworks under default settings.
antipattern.reason: These labels are the statistical mode of solopreneur startup content in training data. Every framework converges to the same outputs without diversity forcing.

rule.statement: Extract market size numbers as unverified_claim fields and require a citable source before they enter the scorecard. "Unknown" is preferable to fabricated confidence.
rule.rationale: LLMs produce round-number TAM figures anchored on frequently seen startup deck numbers. These are confabulation, not research. A fabricated $1B TAM that causes wrong direction is worse than acknowledging unknown and doing targeted research.

antipattern.description: Agent assigns confident market size numbers ($500M, $1B) without any citation in the scoring step.
antipattern.reason: LLMs produce round-number TAM figures anchored on frequently seen startup deck numbers in training data.

rule.statement: Seed the diverge agent with concrete constraints: "10h/week, $0 budget, Python + B2B SaaS experience" beats "find me a profitable idea". Vague goals produce generic output.
rule.rationale: Without explicit skills input, every founder looks like a developer building B2B SaaS in the LLM's default model.

antipattern.description: Agent generates ideas without knowing actual skill depth; output skews toward lexically common categories (SaaS) and undercounts service and info-product opportunities.
antipattern.reason: LLMs fill missing personal context with training-data defaults.

rule.statement: Never let an agent autonomously commit to building an idea. The decision after scoring is a founder decision. Agent output ends at a ranked list with evidence; the commit is human.
rule.rationale: Agent confidence is meaningless on novelty — there is no training-data signal for "is this actually new". Founder must cross-check top ideas against Indie Hackers, Product Hunt, and Google manually before committing resources.

section.p: LLMs systematically over-rate "Speed to MVP" — they think everything is 2 weeks. Hard-code: any idea touching auth/billing/integrations = minimum M.
Pain-point mining without source URLs is fabrication. Force agents to return evidence: [{url, quote}] per idea or reject.
Mode collapse on "AI-powered X" — explicitly forbid prefixes/suffixes (AI, smart, intelligent, GPT) in the prompt to force diverse framing.
Agent confidence is meaningless on novelty — there's no training-data signal for "is this actually new". Always cross-check top idea against Indie Hackers / Product Hunt / Google manually.
LLM-generated TAM/SAM numbers are confabulation. Extract them as unverified_claim and require a citable source before they enter the scorecard.

template.purpose: Idea Discovery Session template: frameworks used, per-idea capture, top-3 selection.
Weekly Idea Capture template for pipeline cron runs.
